page_content:
Prerequisite: Permission of PLCY-School of Public Policy.

Repeatable to: 12 credits if content differs.

Formerly: PUAF349 and PLCY349.

PLCY310 Nonprofit Leadership and Social Innovation in Action (3 Credits) Furthers students understanding of topics in leadership, social innovation, resource development, community mobilization through networks, and the role of policy making in creating change. This course will further students understanding of the creation and leadership of nonprofits, social ventures, governance and boards; strategic planning and partnerships; advocacy and public policy processes; community outreach; working in teams, effective communications, and cross-sector approaches to scaling up social impact.
